verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (TMP21/p24delta1) is a type I single-pass TM protein of the p24 family. The NCBI summary explicitly states it localizes to both the plasma membrane and Golgi cisternae, confirming a documented PM pool alongside a dominant endomembrane (Golgi/ER-Golgi vesicle) residence. It cycles between the ER, Golgi, and plasma membrane as part of its trafficking function. This dual localization — constitutive Golgi/ERGIC residence with a documented PM minority pool — qualifies as contextual/dual_localization rather than yes, because the PM is not the dominant compartment. The extracellular domain (GOLD domain, type I orientation) faces the extracellular space when at the PM, making it accessible to surface-directed binders in that context.
